text.title: cot-techniques Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for cot-techniques. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Set a hard max_depth of 2–3 levels in Tree of Thoughts; with num_branches=3 and max_depth=3 you make up to 9+ API calls per problem — cost explodes without a depth cap.
rule.rationale: ToT is not bounded by default. A 3-branch, 3-level tree makes at least 9 evaluation calls. In a production agent, this can stall a pipeline for minutes and exhaust rate limits.

rule.statement: Use response_format={"type": "json_object"} for thought evaluation in ToT; assign score=0 on JSON parse failure rather than raising an exception.
rule.rationale: Models occasionally produce malformed JSON under structured reasoning load. A crashed evaluation kills the entire ToT run; score=0 gracefully eliminates the path.

rule.statement: Set a wall-clock timeout on ToT runs in agentic pipelines — slow API responses with 9+ branches can stall the pipeline for minutes without a timeout.
rule.rationale: ToT with slow API responses is unbounded in wall time. An agent pool that blocks on one long ToT run starves other tasks.

rule.statement: Cache the decomposition step output in Least-to-Most — if the LLM already decomposed the problem, do not re-decompose on retry; only retry the failed subproblem solution.
rule.rationale: Decomposition calls are expensive. If only one subproblem solution fails, re-running decomposition wastes tokens and risks getting a different (possibly worse) decomposition.

rule.statement: Always try zero-shot CoT ("Think step by step.") first — it solves 70–80% of cases at the cost of only a few extra tokens. Escalate to advanced techniques only after zero-shot fails on your specific problem class.
rule.rationale: Advanced techniques multiply API calls, cost, and latency. Zero-shot is free in terms of additional API calls. Measure before upgrading strategy.

rule.statement: Use o1/o3 (OpenAI) or Claude extended thinking (Anthropic) for problems that natively benefit from deep reasoning — they handle CoT internally and are cheaper than explicit multi-call ToT.
rule.rationale: Extended thinking tokens are billed but not visible in the response text — they represent internal reasoning the model does natively. Running explicit ToT on top of o1 is redundant and expensive.

rule.statement: For self-consistency: sample at temperature=0.7, deduplicate identical outputs before majority vote, and benchmark accuracy gain vs. cost before using more than 5 samples.
rule.rationale: Low-temperature sampling produces identical outputs that artificially inflate one answer's count. Cost multiplies linearly with sample count — 10 samples = 10x the cost of a single call.
